risk_levels.R0: ordinary chat or stable knowledge answer
risk_levels.R2: write durable notes, handoff docs, or artifacts
risk_levels.R4: experiment, build, performance, runtime, external research, or current factual claim
risk_levels.R5: delete, commit, install, login, payment, permission, network/firewall/proxy edit, sensitive transfer, or long-term memory write

global_memory_lane.rule: GLOBAL memory is manual-only; do not infer it from cwd unless the user explicitly requests global memory work.

steps: read memory_summary, _META_INDEX, or router manifest first
select one category index, point index, or outer_retrieval_surface
open only the matching capsule, ERR point, SOL point, or status payload
memory_retrieval_chain.missing_meta_rule: If no meta summary exists, use the smallest available top-level index or manifest as a temporary meta layer, mark the missing meta layer as an adaptation gap, and avoid broad memory/history scans.

dynamic_evaluation_governance.cost_rule: Use the cheapest sufficient route; do not load all memory, all skills, or all history because the dynamic layer is active.
dynamic_evaluation_governance.failure_rule: If dynamic evaluation cannot be completed, mark the final answer as dynamically incomplete or unverified.

mandatory_advisory_control_plane.purpose: make routing, dynamic evaluation, and constitution governance continuously effective without wrapping every tool call
mandatory_advisory_control_plane.cost_rule: Use L0 only for ordinary chat; use lightweight receipts for nontrivial work; run script gates only for ambiguous boundaries, R4/R5, strong claims, memory writes, or explicit hardening.
mandatory_advisory_control_plane.failure_rule: If the control plane was skipped, downgrade final claims and say which boundary was not checked.

search_and_learning_decision_matrix.purpose: avoid closed-door invention and assumption-filled external claims

official_authority_source_search.boundary: Use official or authority sources first for drift-prone public facts; cross-check when practical.

github_open_source_repository_search.boundary: Separate README intent, source-code facts, release or issue evidence, activity signals, and license boundaries.

general_web_cross_check.boundary: Use independent sources when practical; mark source limits when evidence is thin.

source_grounded_learning_intake.boundary: Build a source ledger, classify material, preserve non-applicable boundaries, and require local validation before adoption-success claims.

search_and_learning_decision_matrix.anti_closed_door_rule: For external mechanisms, public repositories, current facts, or unfamiliar methods, do not rely on local reasoning alone. Search or mark the result as unverified.

runtime_enforcement.activation_boundary: The runtime is hard-enforcing only for selected critical boundaries when agent hooks, wrappers, or tool-call paths invoke these scripts before work or tool use. Ordinary tool calls should stay on the advisory control plane.

gate_enforcement_boundary: These gates are hard runtime checks when called through hook/wrapper/tool proxy scripts. They still cannot enforce sandbox-level stops if the caller bypasses the runtime entry scripts.
